edition.dagbeeld: Het VS-Iran conflict escaleert verder: voor de derde nacht op rij luchtaanvallen, terwijl Iran olietankers in de Straat van Hormuz beschiet en Trump een blokkade aankondigt. De energiemarkten en westerse veiligheidsstructuren staan onder druk, met als bijkomend front een aanhoudende cyberoorlog waarbij Rusland Europa bestookt.

article.title: Middle East crisis live: US strikes Iran for third consecutive night; UAE says Tehran has hit tankers in strait of Hormuz
article.summary: Just before the latest strikes Trump said Iran would be hit ‘very hard’; UAE says Iranian cruise missiles hit two oil tankers in strait, killing a crew member and wounding eight

opening.note: Derde nacht VS-Iran-aanvallen; Iran treft tankers in Hormuz, escalatie zet door.
opening.label: breaking

article.title: US military targeted in Iran war phone-tracking campaign
article.summary: Roaming systems and ad tech were used to try to locate American personnel as Tehran attacked forces in the region

front.note: Iran traceerde Amerikaanse militairen via reclametechnologie en roaming-netwerken.
front.label: scoop

article.title: Todd Blanche’s War Against Journalism
article.summary: As the acting attorney general prepares for confirmation hearings, his DOJ is showcasing its willingness to intimidate reporters whose revelations have upset the president.

front.note: Waarnemend AG Blanche richt DOJ in als wapen tegen kritische journalistiek.
front.label: onderzoek

article.title: 1 procent van alle Nederlandse stroom gaat naar één datacenter van Microsoft. Andere bedrijven zwijgen over hun verbruik
article.summary: Microsoft geeft als eerste grote Amerikaanse partij inzicht in het stroomverbruik van zijn datacenters in Nederland. Dat is groot, en groeit de komende jaren bovendien hard. Terwijl andere datacenteruitbaters zwijgen, verhardt de strijd om toekomstige aansluitingen op het stroomnet.

front.note: Microsofts datacenters slokken al 1% van alle Nederlandse stroom op.
front.label: onderzoek

article.title: Russia's FSB blamed for Poland grid attack as UK and EU impose first joint cyber sanctions
article.summary: The allies blamed Center 16, the FSB’s signals intelligence arm, for acts of attempted cyber sabotage targeting Poland’s energy sector and water treatment facilities, alongside “a wide range of malicious cyber activities with growing severity.”

front.note: Russische FSB achter aanval op Pools elektriciteitsnet; EU en VK leggen sancties op.
front.label: scoop

article.title: The Ukraine-Russia War Reaches a New Phase
article.summary: The ground war in Ukraine has stalemated and the crucial fight has shifted to the skies — missiles and interceptors.

front.note: Grondoorlog Oekraïne stokt; luchtoorlog wordt het nieuwe slagveld.
front.label: analyse

article.title: UK police reverses course, launch terror probe into killing of ex-MP Ann Widdecombe
article.summary: Days after saying there was no evidence of terrorism, British authorities say new information has led counter-terrorism officers to take over the investigation into the killing of the former Conservative minister.

front.note: Britse politie opent alsnog terrorisme-onderzoek naar dood oud-parlementslid Widdecombe.
front.label: breaking

article.title: Most UK media reports on June heatwave failed to mention climate crisis
article.summary: Exclusive: Analysis of nearly 2,500 articles finds almost three-quarters made no reference to global heating

front.note: Driekwart van Britse hitteartikelen noemde klimaatcrisis niet — exclusieve analyse.

items.title: Crimea was Russia’s stronghold. Now it’s a vulnerability.

items.title: Deportations by India Cause Tension Along Border with Bangladesh
items.summary: India is driving out Bangladeshi immigrants it says are in the country illegally. Bangladesh is pushing back, leaving people stranded along the border amid increasing tensions between the countries.

items.title: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
items.summary: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.

items.summary: De economisch kleine, maar politiek omstreden handel tussen Europa en de illegale Israëlische nederzettingen op de bezette Westelijke Jordaanoever gaat door. Een grote groep EU-landen wil een verbod, maar Ursula von der Leyen en Berlijn liggen dwars.

items.title: Verdachte in nieuw frituurvet­schandaal blijkt neef van ‘Veluwse vetkoning’
items.summary: De neef van de veroordeelde ‘Veluwse vetkoning’ Cees Bunschoten is nu ook verdachte in een grote biodieselfraudezaak. Gerrit Bunschoten zou via zijn bedrijf Gerbuvet voor miljoenen gefraudeerd hebben met biodiesel. De vetinzamelaar zou vetten bewust verkeerd hebben gelabeld, om ze duurzamer voor te stellen.

items.title: LAPD Regularly Pulled Over Innocent People Because License Plate Readers Flagged Their Cars As Stolen
items.summary: The Los Angeles Police Department let its Flock contract expire over the weekend in part because it was regularly "investigating" and surveilling innocent people.

items.title: VPN service favored by ransomware groups is sanctioned by US
items.summary: The U.S. Treasury Department announced sanctions against First VPN Service (1VPNS) and its Ukrainian administrator for aiding ransomware groups. Separately, a Belarusian man was sanctioned for malware "cryptors."
